title: hymenaea stigonocarpa var. brevipetiolata
intro: Hymenaea stigonocarpa var. brevipetiolata, also known as the Brazilian copaiba tree, is a variant of the Hymenaea stigonocarpa species, a plant native to Brazil. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The oil extracted from its resin is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating skin conditions and respiratory issues. The oil is often used in herbal preparations, such as topical creams and ointments, as well as in traditional medicine, and has been studied for its potential use in modern medicine.
